title: Artemis II crew at ESTEC
summary: The four Artemis II astronauts visited ESTEC, ESA’s technical centre in the Netherlands, on 13 July as the first stop in a series of post-flight visits to the European teams that made their mission possible.

title: What’s Happening in Space Policy July 19-26, 2026

title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary:
The island nation of Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords July 17, becoming the 70th nation to do so in less than six years.
The post Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary:
The maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract was raised from $5.6 billion to $17 billion
The post Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary:
Defense Production Act funding was awarded to Martin Materials Solutions, a small business that makes radiation-resistant cover glass used on spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems
The post Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary:
Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 rocket successfully lifted off on its inaugural flight July 18, becoming the first commercial Indian rocket to reach orbit.
The post Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary:
NASA and Draper have terminated plans for a lunar lander mission after significant delays in development of the spacecraft.
The post NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its structural limits. What they found left them encouraged about the wing’s potential, even when they pushed it past its intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing (SWEET-15) […]

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: Description This composite of images taken by NASA’s Psyche mission shows the crescent of Mars grow as the spacecraft approached the planet for a gravity assist from May 2 to May 15, 2026. title: Serbia, Mauritius Bring Artemis Accords to 70
summary: Serbia and Mauritius have joined the Artemis Accords in the past two days, bringing the number of signatories to 70. Governments of any country are welcome to join the U.S.-led […]
